subject: Alaska.Ai — LinkedIn Carousel No. 16 — 2026-07-24 — 500 to Fewer Than 12. One Office Holds the Pen.
html_body:
Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 16

2026-07-24 · 9 slides · branch tsturg/adoring-shannon-uyvr7n

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (3.39 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: 500 to Fewer Than 12. One Office Holds the Pen.

2 · Paste the post copy

More than 500 comments landed on a North Slope AI data center, and fewer than a dozen backed it.

The state's preliminary nod would hand Stak Energy 715.4 acres 26 miles south of Deadhorse on a 50-year lease, a gas-fired campus that could pull about 1 gigawatt, roughly 30% more than urban Alaska's whole peak grid.

The loudest objection, higher home power bills, is the weakest, because the campus makes its own power on site, off the Railbelt grid that feeds Anchorage and Fairbanks. What actually holds is quieter, 50 years of public land, about 7.1 million cubic yards of gravel on tundra, and 350 to 500 million cubic feet of gas a day. The deck grades them, loudest to strongest.

The comments ran better than 40 to 1 against, and one state agency still holds the signature. So what is that public land honestly worth to lease away for half a century?

#AlaskaEnergy #NorthSlope #DataCenters

3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

Sources, primary first

Automation changes this run (1)

The routine modified its own machinery this run. If a later week looks worse, this dated section is the rollback trail (each set reverts as one commit).
KindChangeRollback
fix
scripts		dedupe_check.py now prints, as the FINAL lines of its text output (after the per-match list and the count summary), a LOUD boxed 'STRONGEST MATCH' summary naming the single top-ranked collision (results[0]: LIKELY DUPLICATE before SOFT OVERLAP, then most shared named entities, then highest jaccard) with its carousel_no, run_date, date-distance in days, verdict, shared-entity count, and jaccard, followed by 'READ THIS ENTRY IN FULL ... Do NOT tail this output'. run() now attaches date_distance to every result. So a `tail` of the output can no longer miss the strongest collision even when many matches push it off the top. Per-match list, verdicts, thresholds, and exit codes are all unchanged; this only restates the existing signal at the end where it cannot be truncated.
why: Run 2026-07-24 top reactive scar: No.16 is a same-docket revisit of No.1 (2026-07-08; same STAK ADL 422741, same '500 wrote in, fewer than 12 said yes' cover). dedupe_check.py CORRECTLY flag		revert the upgrade(2026-07-24) commit (removes the STRONGEST MATCH summary block and the date_distance field; dedupe_check.py reverts to the bare '>> N LIKELY DUPLICATE match(es)' count as its final line)
pending upgrade(2026-07-24) commit (showrunner stages this set)

Aftercare (this is where reach is won)

  • Post Tuesday to Thursday between 8 and 11am Alaska time for the strongest reach, you own the cadence but this is the window
  • Paste the sources comment within 60 seconds of posting so the first comment is live before readers arrive
  • Reply to every comment inside the first 90 minutes, the golden hour, in your own analyst voice, never a canned line
  • If a commenter flags a factual error, check it against the DNR notice at aws.state.ak.us before you concede or push back
  • Log saves and comments at 48 hours and note in the ledger which framing (the sort, the off-grid correction, the pen) drove the thread
